tips_en: Start with RynnBrain 1.1 for the broadest impact—it's a comprehensive embodied foundation model spanning 3D grounding, VLA post-training, and cross-embodiment control, offering the most to digest. Then move to SWE-Pruner Pro if you care about practical LLM efficiency, as its insight of reading pruning signals directly from a frozen backbone's hidden states is elegant and likely generalizable. A unifying thread across all three papers is their use of Qwen-family backbones (Qwen3-Coder, Qwen3.5, Qwen3-VL) and the growing trend of leveraging internal representations or auxiliary lightweight modules to extend foundation model capabilities without retraining the core.
tips_z h: 建议先读 RynnBrain 1.1，因为它覆盖面最广，涵盖 3D 定位、VLA 后训练和跨具身控制，内容最为丰富。如果你关注 LLM 实际部署效率，再读 SWE-Pruner Pro——它直接从冻结主干模型的隐藏状态中读取剪枝信号，这一思路非常优雅且具有可推广性。三篇论文的共同点在于都使用 Qwen 系列模型作为主干（Qwen3-Coder、Qwen3.5、Qwen3-VL），并体现了利用内部表征或轻量辅助模块来扩展基础模型能力、而无需重新训练核心模型的统一趋势。
